code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Determine the primary subject (most frequent as subject)
    subject_counts = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1
    main_subject = max(subject_counts, key=subject_counts.get)

    # Mapping of predicates to readable phrase templates
    # `{}` will be replaced by the object value
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is situated {} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {} feet above sea level",
        "location": "is located in {}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {}",
        "country": "is in {}",
        "language": "where the language is {}",
        "capital": "whose capital is {}",
        "demonym": "people are called {}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {}",
        "hubAirport": "has its hub airport at {}",
        "headquarter": "has its headquarters in {}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {} metres",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "has a runway length of {} feet",
        "runwayLengthMetre": "has a runway length of {} metres",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "its 1st runway surface is {}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "its 2nd runway surface is {}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "its 3rd runway surface is {}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "its 4th runway surface is {}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "its 5th runway surface is {}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "its 1st runway length is {} feet",
        "2ndRunwayLengthFeet": "its 2nd runway length is {} feet",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "its 3rd runway length is {} feet",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "its 4th runway length is {} feet",
        "5thRunwayLengthFeet": "its 5th runway length is {} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "its 1st runway length is {} metres",
        "2ndRunwayLengthMetre": "its 2nd runway length is {} metres",
        "3rdRunwayLengthMetre": "its 3rd runway length is {} metres",
        "4thRunwayLengthMetre": "its 4th runway length is {} metres",
        "5thRunwayLengthMetre": "its 5th runway length is {} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "its 1st runway number is {}",
        "2ndRunwayNumber": "its 2nd runway number is {}",
        "3rdRunwayNumber": "its 3rd runway number is {}",
        "4thRunwayNumber": "its 4th runway number is {}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "its 5th runway number is {}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has IATA code {}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {}",
        "owner": "is owned by {}",
        "leader": "is led by {}",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to the party {}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {}",
        "currency": "currency is {}",
        "regionServed": "serves the region {}",
        "class": "belongs to class {}",
        "division": "belongs to division {}",
        "order": "belongs to order {}",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over {}",
        "countySeat": "county seat is {}",
        "chief": "chief is {}",
        "foundedBy": "founded by {}",
        "foundingYear": "founded in {}",
        "postalCode": "postal code {}",
        "areaCode": "area code {}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "ceremonial county {}",
    }

    # Helper to format a single triple
    def format_triple(t: Triple) -> str:
        tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            return tmpl.format(t.object)
        # fallback: make predicate readable
        readable = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
        return f"{readable} {t.object}"

    # Separate triples about the main subject from the rest
    main_clauses = []
    secondary_clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        clause = format_triple(t)
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            main_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            # Build a subordinate clause linking the secondary subject to its object(s)
            # e.g., "Tirstrup, which is part of the Central Denmark Region, is in Denmark"
            secondary = f"{t.subject}, which {clause}"
            secondary_clauses.append(secondary)

    # Combine clauses
    all_clauses = main_clauses + secondary_clauses
    if not all_clauses:
        return f"{main_subject}."
    # Build a fluent sentence
    sentence = f"{main_subject} " + ", ".join(all_clauses) + "."
    # Clean up possible double spaces
    return " ".join(sentence.split())

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      if not triples:
          return ""
      # The main subject is usually the airport or entity that appears most often as subject
      subject_counts = {}
      for t in triples:
          subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1
      main_subject = max(subject_counts, key=subject_counts.get)

      def fmt_city_served(obj: str) -> str:
          return f"serves the city of {obj}"
      def fmt_elevation(obj: str) -> str:
          # assume metres unless feet is indicated in predicate name
          return f"is situated {obj} metres above sea level"
      def fmt_runway_length(obj: str) -> str:
          return f"its runway is {obj} metres long"
      def fmt_runway_name(obj: str) -> str:
          return f"its runway is named {obj}"
      def fmt_surface(obj: str, order: str) -> str:
          return f"its {order} runway surface is {obj}"
      def fmt_generic(pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
          pred_readable = pred.replace('_', ' ')
          return f"{pred_readable} {obj}"
  
      # Collect clauses, trying to group runway related info

      runway_surface = {}
      runway_length = {}
      runway_name = {}
